schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research

person.wealth_source: Walmart Inc. — youngest surviving son of founder Sam Walton. Walton family collectively owns ~45% of Walmart. Jim's primary operational vehicle is Arvest Bank (44% personal stake, ~$26B assets, ~$2.3B book value Dec 2025 FDIC filing); served as Arvest CEO until 2024, now Chairman of Arvest Bank Group. Walmart board director 2005-present (joined after brother John's 2005 death). Chairs Community Publishers Inc. (newspaper firm).

net_worth.range_note: Forbes Feb 2026 ranks Jim Walton #12 globally at $142.4B (Wikipedia citing Forbes). Bloomberg Billionaires Index maintains a separate real-time figure. Older references (mid-2024 coverage) cited ~$120B. The user brief suggested a $100-115B range but live Forbes data in early 2026 places him materially higher, tracking Walmart share appreciation that drove the Walton family ~$50B surge in early 2026 (per 24/7 Wall St). Jim's net worth is close to his brother Rob's ($143.1B Apr 2026) because their underlying Walmart stakes via Walton Enterprises LLC are roughly comparable.

sources.value_note: Real-time tracker; precise figure not captured in this run

sources.publisher: 24/7 Wall St (Walton family net-worth surge coverage)
sources.value_note: Collective Walton family surge ~$50B in early 2026 — Jim's share tracks this

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Wealth is concentrated in Walmart stock held via Walton Enterprises LLC and family trusts — highly illiquid in practice because coordinated divestment would move the stock and disrupt ~45% family control. Arvest Bank 44% stake is private (book value ~$2.3B; fair value likely higher). Community Publishers Inc. is private. Apply 20% liquidity discount consistent with brother Rob's profile — Walton wealth is structurally locked into the family Walmart control bloc.

cited_events.prorata_method: Divide WFF grants_out by 3 living siblings (Rob, Jim, Alice). Late brother John Walton's estate credited separately to his heirs. Assumes roughly equal contribution share — documented contribution split is not public. Working assumption mirrors Rob's profile for consistency.

cited_events.notes: 100% attributable to Jim Walton (his personal + Lynne's vehicle). Officers/directors Jim C Walton (Co-President) and Lynne M Walton (Co-President as of 09/30/24). Officer compensation in 2024 was $986,004 — higher than RMWF's zero-comp model but not a red flag at 2.9% of grants.

Per Inside Philanthropy Feb 2025 coverage: top 2023 recipients were Bentonville Bella Vista Trailblazer's Association ($5.4M for trail development), Heartland Forward ($5.9M entrepreneurship think tank), Walton Family Foundation ($5.8M intra-family transfer), Peel Compton Foundation ($3.3M).
Founding-year grant-out; foundation tax-exempt as of Sept 2018.

cited_events.donor_entity: Jim Walton (personal) — via SEC Form 4 filing
cited_events.recipient: Undisclosed charitable recipient (most reporting points to Walton Family Foundation or Jim's own foundation vehicle)
cited_events.dedup_note: CRITICAL DEDUP: This $1.2B stock gift in June 2019 was the largest single US philanthropic gift of 2019 per Chronicle of Philanthropy. Recipient not publicly named, but Bloomberg and PND reporting strongly suggest it flowed into the Walton Family Foundation and/or Jim's foundation vehicle (Town Branch Foundation was established 2018 with ~$478M in assets by year-end, suggesting the 2019 TBF growth of ~$25M is not where this went). Most likely classification: TRANSFER INTO WFF. Per dedup rule #2, we count WFF's grants_out (which include this capital's eventual disbursement over subsequent years), NOT the transfer in. Row is listed for provenance only. DO NOT add to observable giving rollup.

cited_events.donor_entity: Town Branch Foundation (Jim and Lynne Walton)
cited_events.recipient: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art (Alice Walton's museum)
cited_events.dedup_note: Captured inside tbf_2019 990-PF grants_out total. Do not double-count. Listed for provenance — confirms Jim has supported Alice's museum, though modestly vs. WFF's $800M Crystal Bridges endowment.

cited_events.status_note: Per Wikipedia, Jim and siblings collectively pledged ~$2B to WFF between 2008-2013. Pledge was a transfer_in to the family foundation, which has since disbursed well above that amount. Pro-rata 1/3 imputed to Jim.
cited_events.dedup_note: Transfer INTO WFF, not grant_out. Per dedup rule #2, we count WFF's grants_out, not the transfers in. Provenance only; not added to observable_giving.

pledges_and_announcements.quote_attribution: Rob Walton speaking for the family: 'I certainly salute them. We have chosen not to do that and to go our own way with our philanthropy.'
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Jim Walton has not signed the Giving Pledge. No members of the Walton family (Jim, Rob, Alice, late brother John's estate) have signed. This is a deliberate family position per 2010 reporting ('no one in state signed up, so far' — NW Arkansas Online Aug 2010). Under scrooge methodology, declining is NOT a red flag per se — more honest than signing-and-not-fulfilling — but it removes a public accountability hook.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: ATTRIBUTION CAUTION: Open-web searches return conflicting numbers between Jim Walton (OpenSecrets ID U0000003347) and his brother Rob Walton (ID U0000003348) — multiple coverage pieces appear to use 'Jim Walton' loosely when the specific $17M figure and the $5M Americans for Prosperity Action gift are documented for Rob in arktimes.com reporting. CONSERVATIVE POSITION: We only log items directly attributable to Jim in this record. Confirmed: (1) Jim Walton gave $500K in May 2024 to defeat an Arkansas K-12 education ballot amendment (pro-school-voucher position), (2) Jim's wife Lynne Walton separately gave ~$12.5M in the 2023-2024 cycle. Jim's own FEC direct-contributions total across the 2023-2024 cycle will need direct FEC bulk pull to confirm vs. press estimates. Pre-2022, neither sibling had given more than $190K in a cycle.
political_giving.lynne_walton_note: Jim's wife Lynne gave ~$12.5M in 2023-2024 per Arkansas Times coverage (Nov 2024). Lynne is also Co-President of the Town Branch Foundation alongside Jim. Spousal political giving is legally separate from Jim's but socially/economically joint. Included here for context, not rolled into Jim's personal political total.

sources.note: Jim C Walton donor detail — direct fetch blocked (403) in this run; OpenSecrets ID U0000003347 confirms separate record from Rob (U0000003348).
Establishes Lynne Walton's $12.5M 2024 cycle total and notes Rob (not Jim) gave $17M. Jim's own cycle total not stated in this piece.
Context on Walmart-heir political-giving trajectory; Jim historically among most conservative givers in the family.

foundations_active.name: Walton Family Foundation Inc
foundations_active.founders: Sam and Helen Walton (original $1,000 + $172M trust at Sam's 1992 death)
foundations_active.officers_compensation: Executive Director Stephanie Cornell $782,636; Education Dir. Romy Drucker $484,508; Environment Dir. Moira McDonald $453,475; General Counsel Christie Yang $446,135. Third-generation Walton family on board (Lukas, Thomas, Carrie Walton Penner, Alice Anne Proietti) with no disclosed officer comp.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants out ($603.5M) vs assets ($6.14B) = 9.8% — comfortably above 5% legal minimum. No LOW_PAYOUT flag.
foundations_active.shared_family_vehicle_note: CRITICAL: Joint-family foundation, not Jim's personal vehicle. Funded collectively by Rob + Jim + Alice + late John's estate + third-generation heirs. For Jim's per-person accounting we credit a 1/3 pro-rata share of grants_out — working assumption, contribution split not public.

foundations_active.name: Town Branch Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: 2024 officer comp $986,004 (non-zero, up from $589K in 2023 and $0 in 2018-2021). Not a red flag at 2.9% of 2024 grants ($34M), but materially different from Rob and Melani Walton Foundation's zero-comp model.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants out ($34.1M) vs assets ($500M) = 6.8% — above 5% minimum but modest relative to peers. 2023 was 9.2%. Trending toward minimum compliance rather than aggressive grantmaking.
foundations_active.low_profile_note: Per Inside Philanthropy Feb 2025: TBF is one of the Waltons' 'lesser-known philanthropies' with 'little if any public profile' and no website. Endowment of ~$500M represents ~0.35% of Jim's current ~$142B net worth — small relative to fortune.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No donor-advised fund activity identified from public sources. Absence-of-evidence only. TBF and WFF grant recipient lists should be parsed for DAF sponsors (Fidelity Charitable, Schwab Charitable, NPT, Vanguard Charitable) to verify no DAF-transfer pattern.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Walton Enterprises LLC is the family's wealth-holding / Walmart-voting vehicle — NOT a philanthropic LLC like CZI or Emerson Collective. Arvest Bank (Jim's primary operational vehicle, 44% stake) is a for-profit commercial bank, NOT philanthropic.

for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.name: Arvest Bank Group
for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.year_joined: 1975 (as head of Walton Enterprises, which held Bank of Bentonville; Arvest CEO through 2024, now Chairman)
for_profit_hybrid_vehicles.methodology_note: Arvest is Jim's primary operational vehicle — NOT philanthropy. Included here to clarify that his day-to-day business life revolves around a commercial bank, not a foundation. Bank stake is illiquid private equity; appreciation would be personal wealth, not charitable capacity.

detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2.

trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.source_quote: Walton family's ~45% Walmart ownership is held through Walton Enterprises LLC and various family trusts — public filings and estate-planning press reference multiple GRATs and dynasty trusts historically used by the family.
trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.classification_note: Walton family known to have used extensive estate-planning trusts (GRATs, dynasty trusts). Specific structure holding Jim's personal wealth not publicly disclosed. Unlike Ellison's pledge letter (which disclosed trust with charitable intent), there is no public statement from Jim committing trust assets to charity. Default assumption: wealth-transfer orientation with minimal charitable remainder.

red_flags.evidence: Jim Walton and all Walton siblings have declined to sign the Giving Pledge. No public signature despite 15+ years of the pledge's existence and Jim's centibillionaire status.
red_flags.severity_note: Informational, not damning. Declining is more honest than signing-and-not-fulfilling. Under scrooge methodology we do NOT penalize declining — only breach.

red_flags.evidence: Jim's pro-rata observable giving (1/3 of WFF grants_out 2019-2024 = ~$1.19B; plus 100% of Town Branch Foundation 2019-2024 = ~$155.3M; combined ~$1.35B) vs. current net worth $142.4B ≈ 0.95% of current NW over 6 years, or 0.16%/yr. Well below the 10% formal norm on liquid wealth. Town Branch endowment of ~$500M is only ~0.35% of Jim's fortune.

Among the three living Walton heirs (Rob, Jim, Alice), Jim has the lowest public charitable profile. Rob has RMWF ($441M assets, $108M 2024 grants, African Parks gifts) and Alice has the Alice Walton Foundation + Crystal Bridges ($800M endowment from WFF). Jim's Town Branch Foundation ($500M assets, $34M 2024 grants) has no website, no public-facing communications, and per Inside Philanthropy 'little if any public profile.' The user brief characterized Jim correctly as 'LEAST publicly philanthropic of the three adult Walton heirs.'

Jim confirmed $500K to defeat a 2024 Arkansas K-12 education ballot amendment (pro-school-voucher); his wife Lynne separately gave ~$12.5M in the 2023-2024 cycle (Lynne is Co-President of Town Branch). Multiple media pieces conflate Jim's and Rob's OpenSecrets records; Jim's personal cycle total requires FEC bulk pull to lock. Household political giving appears large relative to personal foundation grantmaking.

WFF funded collectively by multiple family members; per-sibling contribution splits never publicly disclosed. Pro-rata 1/3 allocation to Jim is a working assumption; his actual share could be materially lower (given he is the youngest, least publicly active sibling) or higher.

red_flags_not_present: LOW_PAYOUT (WFF 9.8%, TBF 6.8% — both above 5% minimum, though TBF trending toward minimum)
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (TBF $986K in 2024 is 2.9% of grants — elevated vs. Rob's zero-comp RMWF but not a red flag threshold)
DAF_TRANSFERS (not confirmed absent — recipient list not yet parsed for DAF sponsors)
GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (never signed, so no breach is possible)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(WFF grants_out 2019-2024) × (1/3 Jim pro-rata) + sum(TBF grants_out 2019-2024 × 100%). WFF 2019-2024 total = $3,584,020,948 × 0.333 = $1,194,673,649. TBF 2019-2024 total = $155,262,869 × 1.00 = $155,262,869. Combined = $1,349,936,518, rounded up slightly for arithmetic. Excludes: transfers INTO WFF (sibling 2008-2013 $2B pledge and 2019 $1.2B stock gift — both transfer_in); political giving; direct-gift rows already inside foundation 990-PF totals (Crystal Bridges $11.5M).

observable_giving_prorata_sensitivity.note: Actual Jim-vs-Rob-vs-Alice contribution split not public. If Jim contributed less than 1/3 (plausible given he has the lowest public profile and wasn't WFF chairman like Rob was at Walmart), observable floor is ~$1.05B at 25%. Middle-case 33% used in primary number.

observable_giving_todo: Pull WFF 990-PF years 2008-2018 to add prior-decade grants_out — the $2B sibling pledge 2008-2013 plus Jim's $1.2B 2019 stock gift should have been disbursing across those years.
Pull TBF 2018 990-PF detail to verify founding-year grants ($7.1M).
Parse WFF + TBF grant recipient lists for DAF sponsors to verify absence of DAF_TRANSFERS flag.
SEC EDGAR Form 4 for Jim Walton (Walmart 13D/G filer) — pull all G-transactions (stock gifts) beyond the 2019 $1.2B event.
FEC bulk pull against Jim C Walton (OpenSecrets ID U0000003347) to disambiguate from Rob's record and confirm/refute the conflated $17M figure.
Verify whether Jim's 2019 $1.2B stock gift went to WFF (default assumption) or a separate vehicle (e.g., a DAF or CRT/CLT) — Bloomberg reporting did not name the recipient.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.20 × $142.4B = $28.48B; tenure cap saturated (~33 years as billionaire). Expected = $2.848B. Strict denominator matching the formal 10% norm.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: No DAF evidence. Zero until recipient-list parsing confirms or denies.
hidden_upper_usd.llc_uplift_note: No philanthropic LLC identified. Walton Enterprises and Arvest Bank are wealth-holding / for-profit vehicles, not giving vehicles.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Jim has the lowest public profile of the Walton heirs — more plausible than his siblings to give anonymously. Apply cohort-median hidden anonymous giving: 0.2% of liquid wealth over 6 years = 0.002 × $28.48B × 6/10 ≈ $342M; rounded conservatively to $250M upper envelope (lower than Rob's because Jim's observable giving is smaller, constraining the anonymous-cohort upper bound).
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Walton family trusts are primarily estate-planning / wealth-transfer vehicles (GRATs, dynasty trusts), not disclosed charitable trusts. Unlike Ellison's pledge letter disclosure, Jim has made no public statement committing trust assets to charity. Zero credit for undisbursed trust assets.
hidden_upper_usd.joint_family_foundation_upside_method: If Jim's true contribution share of WFF is 50% (unlikely given his lowest-profile status), observable rises to ~$1.95B — a $597M uplift vs. middle case. We include this as an upside envelope but do NOT default to it.
hidden_upper_usd.hidden_1p2b_note: Jim's 2019 $1.2B stock gift recipient was never publicly confirmed. If the recipient was WFF (most likely), it's already inside the WFF grants_out pipeline and our pro-rata captures 1/3 of its downstream deployment. If the recipient was a separate vehicle — a CRT, CLT, DAF, or dynasty-adjacent trust — then Jim has an additional ~$1.2B transferred-out that's not in our observable. This is a material uncertainty. Including as upper envelope pending verification.
hidden_upper_usd.total_method: anonymous ($250M) + joint-family upside ($597M) + 1.2B undisclosed-recipient uncertainty ($1.2B). DAF/LLC/offshore/religious/trust all zero. Note: the $1.2B figure should be resolved via SEC Form 4 recipient field + WFF 2019 Schedule B before publication — if confirmed routed to WFF, drop this from hidden envelope.

rollup.tier: Probably_Low (Tier B) — trending toward Tier A Verified Low
rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2. Jim's observable alone clears 47% of expected giving. BUT if the $1.2B hidden-envelope resolves against him (i.e., recipient was WFF and thus already in pipeline), observable drops to ~$1.35B with only ~$847M of realistic hidden uplift = $2.20B total vs $2.85B expected = 77% of expected. He remains Tier B 'Probably Low.' User brief characterized Jim as 'LIKELY Tier A Verified Low' — evidence in this record is more nuanced: his personal vehicle (Town Branch) is small and low-profile (supporting Tier A intuition), but his pro-rata share of WFF is substantial (pushing to Tier B). PRELIMINARY CLASSIFICATION: Tier B Probably Low, with the caveat that a lower pro-rata assumption (25%) or verification that his political household giving displaces charitable (NBER 0.33x) would push toward Tier A.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Jim Walton as the canonical example of 'the least visible Walton heir' — a centibillionaire whose primary life's work has been a for-profit regional bank (Arvest), whose personal philanthropic vehicle (Town Branch Foundation, ~$500M endowment, no website) is dwarfed by his brother Rob's RMWF and his sister Alice's Crystal Bridges/AWF. He has not signed the Giving Pledge (by family position) so cannot be 'in breach.' His 2019 $1.2B Walmart-stock gift is the single biggest public philanthropic marker on his record, but the recipient was never disclosed and is most plausibly WFF — making it a transfer-in, not an out-the-door act of generosity to a specific cause. If Jim's goal were to deploy wealth for public good at scale, the mechanisms exist (TBF could grow 10x in a year from a single Walmart-stock contribution); they have not been used.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula. No pledge breach (never signed). Tenure saturated (~33 years). Red flag penalty 20pts (max) from 4 active red flags. Giving gap 21pts = (1 - 0.474) × 40. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md.

right_of_reply.planned_outreach_route: Town Branch Foundation has no public website — outreach via Walton Enterprises LLC family office + Walton Family Foundation communications + Arvest Bank corporate communications. Three routes to raise visibility and document delivery.
